title: Enforce complete Work Item lifecycle and local cleanup
outOfScope: Deleting arbitrary user branches or worktrees without explicit lifecycle ownership evidence
Changing GitHub branch-protection or reviewer settings

sources.reason: Canonical lifecycle order and finish criteria.
Repository roles, lifecycle closure, and PR ownership rules.
Current post-merge closure and cleanup implementation.
Review finding that the written one-Work-Item lifecycle was not enforced end to end.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix must prevent incomplete Work Item closure without making destructive cleanup of unrelated local state unsafe.

executionDecision.reason: Investigation resolved the implementation boundary: enforce provider-neutral base discovery and make finish/closure states explicit without destructive global cleanup.

preReviewWarnings: Reviewers must confirm that finish output is not presented as closure and that stale unrelated local state is reported, not deleted.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: ai-finish discovers exactly one remote default branch using the same provider-neutral rule as ai-close-work-item and fails closed when discovery is ambiguous.
ai-finish output explicitly states that archive/finish is not lifecycle closure and names the required push, PR merge, and ai-close-work-item steps.
Regression tests cover ambiguous remote default discovery, dedicated branch enforcement, and the non-closure finish message without deleting unrelated branches or worktrees.
The governance complexity threshold is calibrated for the measured lifecycle enforcement change and documented with reviewer impact.
Lifecycle documentation consistently distinguishes finish/archive, PR merge, and final closure, including the safe handling boundary for stale local state.
guidelines: Do not delete local branches or worktrees without verifiable Work Item ownership.
Keep release publication out of scope.

intent.problem: The repository documents a complete Work Item lifecycle, but finish/archive can be mistaken for completion and earlier execution left stale local branches and detached worktrees after PR closure.
constraints: Preserve fail-closed PR and closure behavior.
Do not delete unrelated local branches or worktrees automatically.
Do not change release behavior or publish a version.
intent.rationale: Make lifecycle milestones explicit and make base-branch discovery consistent so operators cannot mistake archive success for final closure or silently lose ownership context.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: finish runs on a dedicated Work Item branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify with existing finish regression tests.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: ambiguous remote default branch discovery fails closed
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify with new base discovery tests.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: finish/archive is distinguished from merged PR closure
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify with finish output and documentation tests.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: unrelated local branches and worktrees are not deleted automatically
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify with closure safety tests and lifecycle documentation.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly instructed to open and continue the new Work Item for the incomplete lifecycle problem.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
